reviews.review_msg: Good professor and very approachable guy. Knowledgeable about the material and corresponds in a timely manner with student queries. Would take again!
I had a lot of fun in 202 but the last project is worth like 50% of your grade and its a group project so just make sure to choose your teammates well.

Class definitely isn't easy. 3 main projects that are graded tough, and they require a lot of effort; it shouldn't be that much of a surprise since it's a filmmaking course and making a short film along with other filmed assignments takes a lot of effort! Labs are mandatory to learn anything in the class, but the professor and TA are very kind!
Dry lectures, the exam was essentially memorizing all the content on all of his lecture slides. If you get incompetent teammates you are screwed because you gotta do everything yourself.
I've had him for three courses. He speaks more of theory then technical aspects.

Mark is a great speaker, knowledgeable, and a very fair marker. The class didn't cover nearly as much sound design/mixing as I expected (a lot is theory) but he makes the lecs interesting. Assignments are easy for the most part. Unlike most SIAT courses, you don't need to "exceed expectations" and overwork yourself to get an A.
Mark is the sweetest prof in SIAT, take all his courses available if possible!

Very informative class. Pay attention to the lecture content and study the slides that he posts online. His lectures were sometimes unrelated to the lab activities, but all in all the labs were lots of fun and some great projects were made.
He is very knowledgable, helpful and resourceful.
